sourceMovie.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Conjuring: Last Rites for the same reason it broke through in 2025: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Michael Chaves keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Conjuring: Last Rites clicked for you, The Conjuring (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Conjuring: Last Rites clicked for you, The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Conjuring: Last Rites?
faq.answer: Try The Conjuring, The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It, Halloween—each shares DNA with The Conjuring: Last Rites while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Conjuring: Last Rites?
faq.answer: The Conjuring: Last Rites (2025) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Conjuring: Last Rites part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Conjuring Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
